name: Grappling Hook
description: A traversal tool that validates a distant anchor, attaches the player to it, then pulls, reels, swings, or redirects momentum through explicit detach rules.
design_notes: Grappling hook quality comes from anchor trust. Designers need to define grappling_anchor_id visibility, target selection, attachment ownership, rope length, collision handling, moving-anchor behavior, and release velocity before building levels around it. The mechanic should make distant space feel reachable without turning every surface into an unintended shortcut.
design_purpose: Let players cross gaps, gain height, or rapidly reposition using readable anchors.
Turn level geometry into traversal opportunities through aiming, commitment, and release timing.
Create expressive routes that combine attachment, swing arcs, detach inputs, and landing recovery.
player_fantasy: The player feels able to seize a distant point and convert it into motion, height, or combat repositioning.

parameters.name: grapple_anchor_distance_max
parameters.description: Maximum distance from player origin to a valid grapple anchor.

parameters.name: anchor_snap_radius_units
parameters.description: Aim assist radius around eligible anchor points.

parameters.name: grapple_reel_velocity
parameters.description: Pull velocity used when reeling toward the anchor.

parameters.name: rope_length_min_units
parameters.description: Shortest allowed rope length before stopping or detaching.

parameters.name: grapple_detach_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Buffered release input accepted during attach, pull, or swing states.

parameters.name: anchor_validation_layers_id
parameters.description: Collision or tag filter used for anchor selection.

edge_cases: Moving anchors must define whether the player inherits anchor velocity and what happens when the anchor is destroyed.
Rope paths around corners need wrapping, collision shortening, or detachment rules.
A grapple that ends inside geometry needs a safe landing probe or rollback point.
Networked grapples need prediction for aim confirmation and reconciliation for attachment position.
common_bugs: Anchor validation uses camera ray hits but movement pulls toward a different physics point.
Rope length goes negative near the anchor and launches the player.
Canceling during attach leaves gravity, input lock, or collision masks in grapple state.
Moving anchors desync because the client stores a world point while the server stores an anchor entity.
balancing_notes: Long anchor distance and strong snap assist make grapple a navigation default; limited anchors make it a route puzzle.
Reel velocity, detach freedom, and fall recovery determine whether grapple is traversal, combat escape, or both.
Moving anchors and enemy anchors need stricter validation because they expand both traversal and encounter solutions.
accessibility_notes: Use strong anchor highlighting and optional snap assist so players can identify valid targets quickly.
Provide input buffering for release and avoid requiring tiny moving-target aim unless alternate controls exist.

implementation_notes.unity: Raycast or spherecast for anchor candidates, store grappling_anchor_id plus local offset, then drive pull or rope simulation from FixedUpdate with swept collision. Replicated play should send the chosen anchor entity rather than only a world position.
implementation_notes.godot: Use direct space-state queries for anchor selection, store attachment state on the character, and update reel or swing velocity in _physics_process. Detach should restore gravity, collision layers, and ordinary input in one state transition.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use trace channels or Gameplay Tags for anchors, replicate the chosen anchor and local offset, and run pull movement through CharacterMovement or a custom movement mode. Prediction should reconcile attach point and rope length together.
implementation_notes.web: Store grapple_state with anchor_id, anchor_local_point, rope_length, and phase, then resolve pull velocity and collision in the fixed simulation step before rendering rope visuals.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Wind Waker
example_games.note: Uses hook-style traversal to cross gaps.

example_games.title: Titanfall 2
example_games.note: Uses grapple movement for high-speed repositioning.
